description: Multiplier skills underneath everything else — DSA, system design, Linux/CLI, networking. Calibrated assuming 250+ LeetCode already solved and basic Linux/Django/React familiarity, so entry-level material is skipped.

topics.title: Applied Data Structures & Algorithms
topics.note: Skipping basic DSA since you have 250+ LeetCode. Focus shifts to applying DSA to real systems.

subtopics.title: Choosing the right data structure under real constraints (memory vs speed tradeoffs)
resources: Real-world case studies of DB indexing choices

subtopics.title: Amortized analysis & practical Big-O in production code (not just interview-speed)
LRU/LFU caches, bloom filters, trie-based autocomplete — implement from scratch
resources: Build your own Redis-lite cache layer

subtopics.title: Graph algorithms for real use: dependency resolution, rate limiters, load balancing
resources: Kubernetes scheduler algorithms as case study

subtopics.title: Concurrency-safe data structures (locks, atomics, lock-free queues)

topics.title: System Design
topics.note: You haven't done formal system design yet — this ramps from fundamentals to production patterns since it's genuinely new.

subtopics.title: Scaling fundamentals: vertical vs horizontal, stateless services, load balancers
resources: 'System Design Interview' by Alex Xu, Vol 1

subtopics.title: Caching strategies (write-through, write-back, cache invalidation)
Database scaling: replication, sharding, partitioning, CAP theorem tradeoffs
Message queues & event-driven architecture (Kafka, RabbitMQ, pub/sub)
API design at scale: rate limiting, idempotency, pagination, versioning
Designing for your own stack: SSE streaming architecture at scale (directly relevant to dynamo's Django API)
resources: Study how OpenAI/Anthropic scale streaming endpoints

subtopics.title: Consistency models, distributed transactions, eventual consistency
Practice: design a production version of dynamo's serving infra (multi-GPU, queueing, autoscaling)

topics.title: Linux + CLI (production depth)
topics.note: Basic CLI assumed known; focus on ops-grade skills relevant to deploying dynamo/agent projects.

subtopics.title: Process management & debugging: systemd, strace, lsof, ps/top/htop under load
Shell scripting for automation (bash functions, traps, argument parsing)
Networking tools: netstat/ss, curl/httpie, tcpdump basics
resources: Practical exercises against your own Django API

subtopics.title: File permissions, users/groups, and securing a production server (SSH hardening, firewall/ufw)
Log management: journalctl, log rotation, centralized logging basics

topics.title: Networking Fundamentals (HTTP, DNS, TCP/IP)
topics.note: Genuine gap area — needed before DevOps/K8s work makes full sense.

subtopics.title: TCP/IP stack basics: how packets actually move (layers, handshakes)
HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2 vs HTTP/3, keep-alive, and how SSE streaming works under the hood
resources: directly applicable to your dynamo SSE API

subtopics.title: DNS resolution, CDNs, and how domain routing works in production
TLS/SSL handshake, HTTPS, and certificate management (Let's Encrypt in practice)
WebSockets vs SSE vs long polling — tradeoffs (relevant since you built SSE streaming already)
resources: Compare against your own dynamo frontend implementation

subtopics.title: Reverse proxies & load balancers in practice: Nginx config, upstream pools
resources: set up in front of your Django API
